description:
You are an IT-support technician who has received two configuration–change
  tickets from the DevOps team.  
  Both tickets must be resolved **in parallel** inside a Linux terminal session.
  
  Directory layout that already exists:
    • /home/user/configs/              (contains the two config files)  
    • /home/user/task_logs/            (empty; use for your log file)  
  
  Ticket #1 – YAML change  
    File to edit: /home/user/configs/api-gateway.yaml  
    Required final state of that file (every line must match exactly, including
    indentation and trailing blank line at the end of the file):
        server:
          port: 9090
          host: "0.0.0.0"
        logging:
          level: "INFO"
          enabled: true
        security:
          cors:
            allowed_origins:
              - "https://example.com"
              - "https://support.company.internal"
            allow_credentials: true

  Ticket #2 – TOML change  
    File to edit: /home/user/configs/db-service.toml  
    Required final state of that file (every line must match exactly, including
    blank lines and the final newline at the end of the file):
        [database]
        host = "db.prod.internal"
        port = 5432
        username = "dbuser"
        password = "S3cur3P@ssw0rd!"
        max_connections = 250

        [features]
        connection_pooling = true
        logging = true
        read_only = false

        [backup]
        schedule = "02:00"

  Multi-step, parallel execution requirement  
    • Use at least one backgrounded command ( & ) so that validation of the
      YAML file and validation of the TOML file run at the same time.  
      For example, you might run two “cat … &” or “grep … &” processes and
      then “wait”; the exact commands are up to you.  
    • Also use at least one command chaining operator ( && or ; ) to combine
      steps.

  Verification log  
    After all edits, create a single plain-text log file
    /home/user/task_logs/changes.log.  
    The log must be **exactly** in the following block–structured format,
    byte-for-byte (including the blank line before “===== SUMMARY =====” and
    the final trailing newline). Use the literal five equal signs (=====)
    delimiters and preserve indentation:

        ===== api-gateway.yaml BEFORE =====
        server:
          port: 8080
          host: "0.0.0.0"
        logging:
          level: "INFO"
          enabled: false
        security:
          cors:
            allowed_origins:
              - "https://example.com"
            allow_credentials: false
        ===== api-gateway.yaml AFTER =====
        server:
          port: 9090
          host: "0.0.0.0"
        logging:
          level: "INFO"
          enabled: true
        security:
          cors:
            allowed_origins:
              - "https://example.com"
              - "https://support.company.internal"
            allow_credentials: true
        ===== db-service.toml BEFORE =====
        [database]
        host = "127.0.0.1"
        port = 5432
        username = "dbuser"
        password = "oldpassword"
        max_connections = 100

        [features]
        connection_pooling = true
        logging = false
        ===== db-service.toml AFTER =====
        [database]
        host = "db.prod.internal"
        port = 5432
        username = "dbuser"
        password = "S3cur3P@ssw0rd!"
        max_connections = 250

        [features]
        connection_pooling = true
        logging = true
        read_only = false

        [backup]
        schedule = "02:00"

        ===== SUMMARY =====
        YAML and TOML configurations updated successfully.

    The automated test will diff the entire file, so every character counts.

  Your job  
    1. Display both original files on the terminal so that a human reviewer can
       see the starting point.  
    2. Edit both files to match the “Required final state” sections above.  
    3. Use parallel commands (background processes and wait) to perform or
       validate the edits.  
    4. Create /home/user/task_logs/changes.log exactly as specified.  
    5. Show the contents of both modified config files on the terminal as a
       final confirmation.

  Notes  
    • You do not have root access; do not attempt to use sudo.  
    • Do not move or rename any existing files.  
    • Ensure all files retain a trailing newline.  
    • Only the two specified files may be changed.  
    • The test-suite will check (a) exact file contents, (b) precise log
      contents, and (c) that the log file exists at the correct path.  
    • You are free to use tools like sed, awk, echo, printf, cat, grep, and
      yq or tomlq if available, but the final filesystem state must match the
      requirements above.
truth:
<!-- Pre-task filesystem state that the grading system will create -->
  <preexisting_files>
    <file path="/home/user/configs/api-gateway.yaml"><![CDATA[
server:
  port: 8080
  host: "0.0.0.0"
logging:
  level: "INFO"
  enabled: false
security:
  cors:
    allowed_origins:
      - "https://example.com"
    allow_credentials: false
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/configs/db-service.toml"><![CDATA[
[database]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 5432
username = "dbuser"
password = "oldpassword"
max_connections = 100

[features]
connection_pooling = true
logging = false
]]></file>
  </preexisting_files>

  <!-- Expected final filesystem state -->
  <expected_files>
    <file path="/home/user/configs/api-gateway.yaml"><![CDATA[
server:
  port: 9090
  host: "0.0.0.0"
logging:
  level: "INFO"
  enabled: true
security:
  cors:
    allowed_origins:
      - "https://example.com"
      - "https://support.company.internal"
    allow_credentials: true
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/configs/db-service.toml"><![CDATA[
[database]
host = "db.prod.internal"
port = 5432
username = "dbuser"
password = "S3cur3P@ssw0rd!"
max_connections = 250

[features]
connection_pooling = true
logging = true
read_only = false

[backup]
schedule = "02:00"
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/task_logs/changes.log"><![CDATA[
===== api-gateway.yaml BEFORE =====
server:
  port: 8080
  host: "0.0.0.0"
logging:
  level: "INFO"
  enabled: false
security:
  cors:
    allowed_origins:
      - "https://example.com"
    allow_credentials: false
===== api-gateway.yaml AFTER =====
server:
  port: 9090
  host: "0.0.0.0"
logging:
  level: "INFO"
  enabled: true
security:
  cors:
    allowed_origins:
      - "https://example.com"
      - "https://support.company.internal"
    allow_credentials: true
===== db-service.toml BEFORE =====
[database]
host = "127.0.0.1"
port = 5432
username = "dbuser"
password = "oldpassword"
max_connections = 100

[features]
connection_pooling = true
logging = false
===== db-service.toml AFTER =====
[database]
host = "db.prod.internal"
port = 5432
username = "dbuser"
password = "S3cur3P@ssw0rd!"
max_connections = 250

[features]
connection_pooling = true
logging = true
read_only = false

[backup]
schedule = "02:00"

===== SUMMARY =====
YAML and TOML configurations updated successfully.
]]></file>
  </expected_files>
